# artifactservecli

> A Bun + TypeScript CLI that uploads a file to any S3-compatible storage (printing the public download URL as a terminal QR code), or serves a file over the local network with a QR code — no cloud config needed for serving.

Runtime: **Bun** (not node). Source is TypeScript ESM run directly; there is no build step.

## Commands

```bash
# Upload to S3-compatible storage, print URL + QR code
bun src/index.ts <file> [options]

# Serve a file over the LAN until Ctrl+C (no cloud config needed)
bun src/index.ts serve <file> [options]

# If installed globally (bin name)
artifactservecli <file> [options]
artifactservecli serve <file> [options]
```

## Upload options

- `-b, --bucket <name>` — bucket name (env: `ASC_BUCKET`)
- `-u, --public-url <url>` — public URL prefix (env: `ASC_PUBLIC_URL`)
- `-e, --endpoint <url>` — S3 endpoint URL (env: `ASC_ENDPOINT`, omit for AWS S3)
- `-r, --region <name>` — S3 region (env: `ASC_REGION`, default: `auto`)
- `-n, --name <name>` — remote object name (default: `{name}-{timestamp}{ext}`)
- `--no-qr` — skip QR code
- `-h, --help` — show help

## Serve options

- `-p, --port <n>` — port to bind (default: 8787, auto-falls back to next free port up to +20)
- `--no-qr` — skip QR code

## Configuration (upload only)

Set in a `.env` file in the **current working directory** (gitignored), or pass as flags:

```
ASC_ACCESS_KEY_ID=...
ASC_SECRET_ACCESS_KEY=...
ASC_BUCKET=...
ASC_PUBLIC_URL=https://...
ASC_ENDPOINT=...   # optional; omit for AWS S3
ASC_REGION=...     # optional; default: auto
```

`serve` needs none of these — it only reads the file and binds a local port.

## Examples

```bash
# Upload an APK and get a scannable download link
artifactservecli ./app-release.apk

# Upload with a fixed remote name, no QR
artifactservecli ./build.ipa -n latest.ipa --no-qr

# Share a file with someone on the same Wi-Fi
artifactservecli serve ./video.mp4 -p 9000
```

## Notes for agents

- Always invoke with `bun` (per project convention); do not use `node` or `npm`.
- Typecheck with `tsc --noEmit` (or `bun run check`). No tests, no emit.
- The S3 client sets checksum calc to `WHEN_REQUIRED` — required because some S3-compatible providers (e.g. Cloudflare R2) reject the AWS SDK's default flexible checksum headers; do not remove.
- Source files import siblings with `.js` extensions despite being `.ts` (bundler moduleResolution + ESM).
